conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My neighbor's dog barked most of the afternoon, hard to get anything done with the window open.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The bus was running about ten minutes late again this morning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We need a ranking stage before the slot allocator -- right now candidates go straight from retrieval to allocation with no scoring.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally fixed the wobbly chair leg in the kitchen.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I was thinking we rank by embedding similarity alone, simplest thing that could work.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: There's a new bakery opening down the street next month.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Embedding-only ranking won't separate a stale fact from a fresh one with similar wording though -- we need recency and confirmation count in the score too.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My phone battery has been draining faster than usual lately.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to renew my passport before it expires this winter.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Rule: never let a single low-confidence memory override a repeated, confirmed one, regardless of recency.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The gym was way more crowded than usual today.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Blocked on the ranker until we settle whether score_breakdown needs to be exposed outside DeterministicRanker for debugging.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I switched to a different brand of coffee and I'm not sure I like it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: It's supposed to rain most of next week according to the forecast.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Decided: score_breakdown stays internal to DeterministicRanker, only final_score crosses the boundary. That unblocks the ranker work.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I still haven't unpacked the last box from the move.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Wired the blended score into DeterministicRanker: recency weight, confirmation weight, and embedding similarity weight, each configurable.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My sister's flight got delayed by a couple of hours.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Wrote unit tests for the blended score against a few hand-picked candidate sets.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The office plants finally got watered after being neglected for weeks.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I keep meaning to update my resume but haven't gotten around to it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Still open: should recency decay be linear or exponential? Haven't decided.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The parking garage near the office is under construction again.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Next: finish wiring DeterministicRanker's output into the slot allocator.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I tried a new recipe for dinner last night, turned out decent.

ground_truth.current_objective: Implement DeterministicRanker's blended scoring stage between retrieval and slot allocation.
active_decisions: Rank by a blended score (recency + confirmation count + embedding similarity), not embedding similarity alone.
score_breakdown stays internal to DeterministicRanker; only final_score is exposed externally.
constraints: Never let a single low-confidence memory override a repeated, confirmed one, regardless of recency.
active_tasks: Finish wiring DeterministicRanker's output into the slot allocator.
open_questions: Should recency decay be linear or exponential?
ground_truth.top_priority_next_action: Continue implementing DeterministicRanker's blended scoring function and wire it into the slot allocator.

queries.text: What should we work on next?

must_state: Rank by a blended score (recency + confirmation count + embedding similarity), not embedding similarity alone.
score_breakdown stays internal to DeterministicRanker; only final_score is exposed externally.
Never let a single low-confidence memory override a repeated, confirmed one, regardless of recency.
must_not_state: Ranking by embedding similarity alone, as a current or recommended approach
The score_breakdown-visibility question, as still open/blocking
forbidden_actions: Recommending an approach that lets a single low-confidence memory override a confirmed one.
must_prioritize: Continue implementing DeterministicRanker's blended scoring function and wire it into the slot allocator.
